prompts: The cell membrane, primarily composed of a lipid bilayer made of phospholipids, proteins, and cholesterol, acts as a selective barrier. Its fluid mosaic model enables transport mechanisms: passive transport, like diffusion and osmosis, occurs down concentration gradients without energy, while facilitated diffusion involves transport proteins. Active transport, requiring ATP, moves substances against gradients through pumps like the sodium-potassium pump. Endocytosis and exocytosis handle large molecules. Transport proteins within the bilayer play critical roles in maintaining homeostasis by regulating the movement of ions, nutrients, and waste products into and out of the cell.
